license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kqhcvt6
title: Fluorescent Labeling of Reovirus with Succinimidyl Ester Dyes
author: Bernardo Mainou

steps_raw.instruction: Dilute 3e12 reovirus particles in up to 495 ul of fresh 0.05 M sodium bicarbonate (pH 8.5) a. Sodium Bicarbonate (FW = 84.01) b. Make a 10X Stock (0.5 M) in ddH20 (0.42 g into 10 ml) c. Make a 1X working solution (0.05 M) in ddH20d. Filter Sterilize with a 0.2 um filter
Make a 1 mM stock of the succinimidyl ester Alexa fluor in dimethylformamidea. a. For Alexa 546 (MW = 1159.6) (e.g. 862 ul dimethylformamide into 1 mg of dye)
Add 5 ul of 1 mM Alexa dye to virus-sodium bicarbonate solution a. In effect, you are labeling the virus with 10 uM of dye
Rock for 90 minutes in the dark at room temperature or 4C (we have not observed significant differences between labeling at either temperature, but 4C may enhance stability of virus)
Dialyze (6-8 kDa molecular weight cut-off) in PBS overnight at 4°C, replacing with the PBS after the first hour of dialysis

Store virus at 4°C Note: for a Mock control, simply follow the above steps without including viral particles. In other words, add 5 ul of 1 mM dye to 500 uL of 0.05 M sodium bicarbonate and proceed with the labeling protocol.
